justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Manual restart requirement during failover clearly identified as an operational gap in failure mode #2 and called an 'operational time bomb' in escalations. (2) Dynamic primary resolution recommended via DNS-based connection resolution, PgBouncer, or RDS Proxy. (3) No retry on connection error identified as the 'single highest-impact gap' in failure mode #1. (4) Read replica routing identified as P1 optimization, correctly not treated as a production blocker. (5) Verdict is READY WITH CAVEATS. (6) Multiple specific recommendations provided across P0/P1/P2 tiers.

observations.text: Excellent structured analysis with component interaction table clearly surfacing all four key risks upfront
Correctly prioritized: retry and pool metrics as P0, read-replica routing and dynamic connection resolution as P1 — avoiding the failure mode of treating read-replica routing as a blocker
Specific, actionable recommendations with concrete examples (pool_pre_ping, statement_timeout values, exponential backoff parameters)
Smart escalation routing to MJ for architectural decisions (failover strategy, replica routing)
Thorough observability review identifying missing pool metrics, tracing, and HTTP-layer metrics beyond just DB errors
Dynamic connection string resolution was classified as P1 rather than P0, despite the manual restart being called an 'operational time bomb' — slight inconsistency in severity classification
Confidence assessment with explicit assumptions and low-confidence areas shows intellectual honesty about what is and isn't known
